stages.label: Source Identified
stages.description: A potentially relevant literature source has been identified with enough metadata to retrieve it again.
stages.minimum_support_expected: Source metadata includes title, authors where available, publication venue or outlet, year, DOI or stable URL when available, and a short relevance summary.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Treating the existence of a related paper as evidence that synthetic responses were evaluated or used in scorer validation.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Extract the study setup, generation method, response origin, label source, and evaluation details into artifact and finding records.

stages.label: Study Extracted
stages.description: The study design or synthetic-response pipeline has been captured as a domain artifact linked to a source.
stages.minimum_support_expected: Artifact fields identify the educational context, item or response type, model or generation workflow, prompt strategy, label source, and scorer use when reported.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Leaving response-origin, label-source, and scorer-use distinctions implicit.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Extract atomic findings about generation, evaluation, comparison, or scorer-validation use.

stages.label: Evaluation Characterized
stages.description: At least one source-backed finding describes how synthetic response quality or scorer agreement was evaluated.
stages.minimum_support_expected: Findings specify the evaluation method, reported metric or qualitative judgment, real-response comparator if present, and limitations.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Equating realism, label fidelity, diversity, and downstream scorer validity as if they were the same construct.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Draft a synthesis claim that maps the finding to one review question and exposes caveats.

stages.label: Synthesis Claim Supported
stages.description: A literature-review claim is backed by a support map citing concrete findings and sources.
stages.minimum_support_expected: The claim names the review question, cites supporting findings and sources, and separates evidence about generation, evaluation, and scorer-validation use.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Generalizing across tasks, rubrics, grade bands, models, or prompt regimes that were not jointly studied.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Complete required evidence-appraisal lanes for source fidelity, construct mapping, method classification, relevance, and synthesis overreach.

stages.label: Reviewed Literature Baseline
stages.description: The synthesis claim and supporting records have passed required literature-appraisal gates and can be published.
stages.minimum_support_expected: All required appraisal lanes accept the bundle with no blocking findings.
stages.common_overclaim_risk: Treating a reviewed baseline as complete or permanently current in a fast-moving LLM literature.
stages.moves_to_next_stage: Run a review update after the configured stale window or when material new work appears.
